message:
refactor(cli): tighten --refresh error paths and validation order

Three small fresh-eyes fixes on `refresh_index_inline` and its search-
subcommand call site:

1. `last_total` sentinel was `0`, which collided with the legitimate
   "indexer hasn't set a total yet" atomic value. Align it with the
   other two sentinels (`usize::MAX`) so the first real progress
   update is always distinguishable from the initial state and gets
   printed.

2. Panic payloads from `JoinHandle::join` were discarded via
   `Err(_panic)`. Recover the string contents (`String` /
   `&'static str` cover ~every panic message), print it in the
   warning, and attach it to the tracing event. Unknown payload
   types fall back to a placeholder so we don't lose the
   "panicked" signal.

3. Search: run clap flag-conflict validation (two-tier group,
   daemon/no-daemon, reranker-without-rerank) *before* kicking off
   the ~30s incremental refresh, not after. `cass search --refresh
   --two-tier --fast-only` used to burn a full indexer pass before
   rejecting the bad flag combo.

No behavioural change on the happy path; this only improves UX on
misuse and on indexer panics, and eliminates the sentinel collision.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
